questions.question: The Abbasid Caliphate declined by the end of the _____ century.
questions.explanation: The Abbasid Caliphate declined by the end of the 9th century and disintegrated into several states ruled by the Turks.

questions.question: The two important Turkish states that emerged after the decline of the Abbasid Caliphate were _____ and Ghor.
questions.explanation: After the decline of the Abbasid Caliphate, two important states that emerged were Ghazni and Ghor.

questions.question: Mahmud of Ghazni invaded India _____ times in 25 years.
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni invaded India 17 times between 1001 and 1025 CE.

questions.question: Mahmud of Ghazni's invasions of India took place from 1001 to _____ CE.
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni invaded India 17 times over a period of 25 years from 1001 to 1025 CE.

questions.question: _____ was the ruler of Punjab who was defeated by Mahmud of Ghazni and burnt himself to death.
questions.explanation: Jaipal, the ruler of Punjab, was defeated by Mahmud of Ghazni and burnt himself to death in shame.

questions.question: Anandapal, who formed an alliance against Mahmud of Ghazni, was the son of _____.
questions.explanation: Anandapal was Jaipal's son. He formed an alliance with other rulers to fight Mahmud but was defeated.

questions.question: Mahmud of Ghazni's most famous raid was on the _____ Temple in Kathiawar.
questions.explanation: The most famous of Mahmud's attacks on temple towns was the raid on the Somnath Temple in Kathiawar, where about 50,000 soldiers died defending it.

questions.question: About _____ soldiers died defending the Somnath Temple against Mahmud of Ghazni.
questions.explanation: About 50,000 soldiers died defending the Somnath Temple when Mahmud of Ghazni attacked it.

questions.question: _____ was the only Indian province that Mahmud of Ghazni annexed.
questions.explanation: Despite 17 invasions, Punjab was the only Indian province that Mahmud of Ghazni actually annexed to his kingdom.

questions.question: Mahmud of Ghazni was known as the '_____' and was also a patron of art and learning.
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni was known as the 'idol breaker' because of his destruction of Hindu temples, but he was also a patron of art and learning.

questions.question: Firdausi, who wrote the _____, was patronized by Mahmud of Ghazni.
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni patronized Firdausi, who wrote the famous Shahnama, a great Persian epic.

questions.question: Muhammad Ghori conquered India towards the end of the _____ century.
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ghori, the ruler of Ghor, conquered India towards the end of the 12th century.

questions.question: The First Battle of Tarain was fought in the year _____.
questions.explanation: The First Battle of Tarain took place in 1191, in which Prithviraj Chauhan defeated Muhammad Ghori.

questions.question: In the Second Battle of Tarain (1192), Muhammad Ghori defeated _____.
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ghori defeated Prithviraj Chauhan in the Second Battle of Tarain in 1192, ending Rajput rule.

questions.question: Raja _____ of Kanauj refused to help Prithviraj Chauhan against Muhammad Ghori.
questions.explanation: Raja Jaichandra of Kanauj refused to help Prithviraj Chauhan, which contributed to his defeat in the Second Battle of Tarain.

questions.question: Muhammad Ghori appointed _____ as his viceroy in India.
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ghori appointed Qutbuddin Aibak as his viceroy to administer his Indian territories.

questions.question: Muhammad bin Bakhtiar Khilji destroyed Buddhist monasteries and libraries in _____ and overthrew the Sena dynasty of Bengal.
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Bakhtiar Khilji destroyed Buddhist monasteries and libraries in Bihar and also overthrew the Sena dynasty of Bengal.

questions.question: The Delhi Sultanate lasted from 1206 to _____ CE.
questions.explanation: The Delhi Sultanate lasted from 1206 to 1526 CE, and its rulers were called sultans.

questions.question: Qutbuddin Aibak, who ruled from 1206 to 1210, was a former _____ and was called 'Lakhbaksh'.
questions.explanation: Qutbuddin Aibak was a former slave who became the first sultan of the Delhi Sultanate. He was called 'Lakhbaksh' for his generosity.

questions.question: _____ is considered the true founder of the Delhi Sultanate and was the son-in-law of Qutbuddin Aibak.
questions.explanation: Iltutmish (1210-1236) was the son-in-law of Qutbuddin Aibak and is considered the true founder of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.question: Iltutmish saved India from the Mongol invasion by refusing shelter to the Shah of _____ .
questions.explanation: Iltutmish wisely refused to give shelter to the Shah of Persia, thereby saving India from a possible invasion by the Mongols under Chenghiz Khan.

questions.question: Iltutmish was recognized as the rightful ruler of Delhi by the Caliph of _____.
questions.explanation: Iltutmish received recognition from the Caliph of Baghdad, which gave his rule religious legitimacy.

questions.question: The Qutb Minar was started by Qutbuddin Aibak and completed by _____.
questions.explanation: Qutbuddin Aibak started the construction of the Qutb Minar, and it was completed by his successor Iltutmish.

questions.question: Razia Sultan ruled the Delhi Sultanate from 1236 to _____ and was killed in 1240.
questions.explanation: Razia Sultan, daughter of Iltutmish, ruled from 1236 to 1239. She married Altunia but was killed in 1240.

questions.question: Ghiyasuddin Balban, who ruled from 1266 to 1286, believed in the theory of _____ kingship.
questions.explanation: Ghiyasuddin Balban believed in the theory of divine kingship. He never smiled and made the monarchy absolute in authority.
